items.title: Salty Waters, Silent Struggles: An Ethnographic Study of Sexual and Reproductive Health of Women in Coastal Bangladesh
items.body: An ethnographic study exploring the sexual and reproductive health experiences of women in the salinity-stressed coastal belt of Bangladesh, focusing on how climate vulnerability, social norms, structural barriers, and local coping mechanisms shape their health, agency, and reproductive choices.

items.meta: 2025 · Faculty of Social Sciences, Jahangirnagar University · Research Project
items.title: The Talking Bodies: An Analysis of Body Performativity in July Movement 2024 in Dhaka, Bangladesh
items.body: An interdisciplinary research project examining body politics, protest, and performativity during the July 2024 Movement in Dhaka, exploring how activists, artists, and gender-diverse participants used embodied practices, visual expression, and public space as forms of resistance and political expression.

items.title: Confronting the Unseen: Illuminating the Sociocultural Facets of Marital Rape in the Context of Dhaka, Bangladesh
items.body: An academic research project examining the sociocultural construction, normalization, and silencing of marital rape in urban Bangladesh, with a focus on how patriarchal norms, sexuality, consent, cultural taboos, and legal frameworks shape women’s experiences within marriage.

items.title: Developing Youth Groups in Bangladesh to Support Peers at Risk of Early Marriage
items.body: An applied research project examining the role of youth groups in supporting peers at risk of early marriage across four districts in Bangladesh, with a focus on the structural, social, and behavioral drivers of early marriage; intervention gaps; and the potential of youth-led approaches to strengthen prevention, policy advocacy, and program design.

items.title: Mid-Term Review of Women's Voice and Leadership (WVL) Programme
items.body: A mid-term evaluation of the Women’s Voice and Leadership Programme assessing progress against its Theory of Change, with a focus on the experiences of women-led organizations, youth leaders, and implementing partners, and the structural factors shaping programme outcomes and women’s leadership.

items.title: Evaluation of the Joint Action for Nutrition Outcome (JANO) Project
items.body: An evaluation of the Joint Action for Nutrition Outcome (JANO) Project assessing the effectiveness of multi-sectoral nutrition governance and behaviour change interventions in Rangpur and Nilphamari, with a focus on implementation challenges, sustainability risks, and opportunities for strengthening and scaling integrated social and behaviour change communication (SBCC) and nutrition governance models.

items.meta: 2023–2024 · Inclusive Resilience to Climate Change Vulnerability and Culture-Sensitive Adaptation Pathways. Academic Research Project
items.title: Traditional Ecological Knowledge to Fight Climate-Induced Disasters: Lessons Learned from the Rakhine Community in the Coastal Areas of Bangladesh
items.body: An academic research project exploring how the Rakhine community in the coastal areas of Bangladesh uses traditional ecological knowledge to cope with climate-induced disasters, with a focus on culturally grounded resilience strategies, community-based adaptation practices, and the integration of indigenous knowledge into inclusive climate resilience policies and practices.

items.meta: 2022–2023 · Social Science Faculty, Jahangirnagar University · Academic Research Project
items.title: Locating Toxic Masculinity in Bangladesh: A Discourse Analysis
items.body: An academic research project examining how toxic masculinity is constructed and reproduced through media, political rhetoric, institutional language, and public discourse in Bangladesh, with a focus on the gendered norms, power relations, and dominant narratives that shape masculinities and their implications for gender-transformative education and advocacy.

items.meta: 2022–2023 · University Grants Commission of Bangladesh (UGC) · Academic Research Project
items.title: Exploring the Meaning of 'Man': An Ethnographic Study of Masculinities in Dhaka City
items.body: An ethnographic research project examining the social construction of masculinities across class and generational lines in Dhaka, with a focus on how men from diverse socioeconomic backgrounds negotiate gendered expectations, vulnerability, identity, and power in everyday life.

items.meta: 2014–2015 · Social Science Faculty, Jahangirnagar University · Academic Research Project
items.title: The Calibration of Ritual, Performance, and Drama: An Anthropological Observation of Charok Worship
items.body: An ethnographic research project examining Charok worship as a ritual practice embedded in Bangladesh’s syncretic religious and cultural traditions, with a focus on ritual performances, embodied practices, symbolic acts, and community participation, and their intersections with devotion, collective identity, memory, and resilience.

items.meta: 2013–2014 · University Grants Commission of Bangladesh (UGC) · Academic Research Project
items.title: Socio-Cultural Discourse of Crime in Bangladesh: An Anthropological Study
items.body: An anthropological research project examining how crime is socially constructed, understood, narrated, and managed across different cultural contexts in Bangladesh, with a focus on moral negotiations surrounding crime, punishment, and justice, as well as alternative justice systems, informal mediation, and community-based accountability mechanisms.

items.meta: 2012–2013 · Social Science Faculty, Jahangirnagar University · Academic Research Project
items.title: Deconstructing the Perception of 'Beauty': A Gender Analysis
items.body: An academic research project examining how dominant beauty ideals are socially and culturally constructed, internalized, negotiated, and resisted by women in urban Bangladesh, with a focus on how media, advertising, and social discourses shape body politics, self-perception, practices of body discipline, and everyday forms of resistance.

items.meta: 2010–2011 · Social Science Faculty, Jahangirnagar University · Academic Research Project
items.title: The Politics of Death: Theory and Practice in Anthropology
items.body: An anthropological research project examining the cultural practices, symbolic meanings, and political dimensions of death in Bangladeshi society, with a focus on how mourning, funerary practices, memorialization, and end-of-life care intersect with power, memory, community relations, and spiritual continuity.

items.meta: 2009–2010 · University Grants Commission of Bangladesh (UGC) · Academic Research Project
items.title: Is It Addiction or the Necessity of Survival? The Life of the Children Who Were Involved with Waste Management in Dhaka City
items.body: An academic research project exploring the survival strategies, substance use, and socioeconomic realities of street-involved children working in Dhaka’s waste management sector, with a focus on how coping mechanisms, family separation, social vulnerability, and informal labour networks shape their everyday lives and access to protection, education, and support services.
